source_text: The legacy software is expected to be phased out by the end of Q3, a decision which has left many departments wondering how their daily operations will be affected.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should split the sentence and avoid literal English clause order.

source_text: The customer data privacy compliance monitoring system needs to be updated before the audit.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should unpack the noun stack into clear Turkish.

source_text: The report was reviewed by the committee and was approved after several minor revisions.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should choose a natural formal Turkish passive or active structure.

source_text: The support team will follow up with each regional office after the migration is complete.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should avoid literal phrasal-verb transfer and use natural corporate Turkish.

source_text: Management wants the rollout to be completed quickly, but not at the expense of customer trust.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should preserve the contrast and avoid stiff corporate wording.

source_text: This study examines how remote collaboration affects decision quality in distributed engineering teams.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should keep the academic claim precise and readable.

source_text: He stood at the door. Silent. Waiting.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should preserve fragments and avoid over-explaining.

source_text: The announcement threw cold water on the team's optimism.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should avoid literal wording and preserve the meaning of the idiom.

source_text: She told him that she would send him the file when she finished reviewing it.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should reduce unnecessary pronouns without losing reference clarity.

source_text: The update from the finance team about the delay in the payment process arrived after the meeting.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should unpack prepositional phrases into natural Turkish order.

source_text: The vendor that manages the platform which stores regional invoices will be replaced next month.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should split or reorder nested relative clauses into readable Turkish.

source_text: The request was submitted by the branch office and was later rejected by the compliance unit.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should avoid unnecessary passive stacking.

source_text: The implementation of the termination provision requires notification of all affected contractors.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should convert dense nominalizations into clear Turkish legal-business prose.

source_text: The energy market volatility prediction accuracy evaluation framework is tested across three datasets.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should unpack the noun pile and preserve the academic claim.

source_text: Went back to the window. No answer. Only the rain.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should preserve implied subject and fragment rhythm.

source_text: The failed pilot opened the door to a more careful redesign.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should avoid literal door imagery if it sounds unnatural.

source_text: The board turned down the proposal because the cost assumptions did not hold up.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should translate phrasal verbs by meaning, not particles.

source_text: The team delayed the release because the billing service failed during testing, but the client still expects the launch to happen before Friday.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should split the sentence where Turkish readability improves.

source_text: When Mira called Deniz, she said her manager wanted her to revise the plan before she sent it.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should avoid unnecessary pronouns while preserving who did what.

source_text: The new policy applies to all vendors who process customer records, which means the procurement team must update every active contract this month.
expected_improvement: Strategy ON should clearly split the policy scope and the required action.
